reply:
[[BLOCK:srt_37]]
Speaker 1: I think you’ll hear many students say the same thing—that what they learn in class today can be applied directly to their jobs the very next day.

[[BLOCK:srt_38]]
Speaker 1: And just as importantly, they bring their real-world experience back into the classroom to share with others.

[[BLOCK:srt_39]]
Speaker 1: So ultimately, it comes down to this: can what you learn today move your career forward tomorrow?

[[BLOCK:srt_40]]
Speaker 3: Excellent.

[[BLOCK:srt_41]]
Speaker 3: Could you share one or two alumni stories that best reflect your university’s strong career focus?

[[BLOCK:srt_42]]
Speaker 3: Perhaps we can start with an example from the field of education.

[[BLOCK:srt_43]]
Speaker 3: Would that be all right?

[[BLOCK:srt_44]]
Speaker 2: May I go first?

[[BLOCK:srt_45]]
Speaker 2: Please.

[[BLOCK:srt_46]]
Speaker 2: Then I’ll begin with one of our most distinguished alumni—Richard Rosenberg (Richard Rosenberg). He served as CEO and Chairman of Bank of America, and I myself once worked at Bank of America.

[[BLOCK:srt_47]]
Speaker 2: What’s remarkable is that he often asked, how does someone who is the first in his family to attend college rise all the way to become Chairman of a global financial institution like Bank of America?

[[BLOCK:srt_48]]
Speaker 2: His answer was actually quite simple—education.
